scheme: National Action Plan on Climate Change
text: India's NAPCC launched in 2008 has 8 National Missions: Solar, Enhanced Energy Efficiency, Sustainable Habitat, Water, Sustaining Himalayan Ecosystem, Green India, Sustainable Agriculture, Strategic Knowledge on Climate Change. India committed to net-zero emissions by 2070 at COP26 (Glasgow). India's NDC: 50% non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.

International Solar Alliance (ISA) co-founded by India and France in 2015. Headquartered in Gurugram, India. 116 member countries. Aims to deploy 1,000 GW solar energy by 2030 and mobilize $1 trillion investments. India's solar capacity grew from 2.6 GW (2014) to 73+ GW (2024). Target: 280 GW solar by 2030.

Swachh Bharat Mission (launched Oct 2014) built 12+ crore toilets, declared India Open Defecation Free (ODF) in 2019. Phase 2 focuses on solid/liquid waste management. Environmental impact: reduced groundwater contamination, improved sanitation leading to fewer waterborne diseases. Budget: Rs 1.4 lakh crore across phases.

NCAP launched in 2019 targets 40% reduction in PM2.5 and PM10 concentrations by 2025-26 in 131 non-attainment cities. Budget: Rs 10,566 crore. Measures include electric vehicles, cleaner fuels (BS-VI), industrial emission controls, GRAP in Delhi-NCR, stubble burning management. Delhi's AQI crisis remains a major challenge every winter.

CAMPA (Compensatory Afforestation Fund Management and Planning Authority) manages funds collected from forest land diversion. Corpus: Rs 48,000+ crore. Forest cover increased from 21.05% (2011) to 21.71% (2021) of geographic area. India pledged to create additional carbon sink of 2.5-3 billion tonnes CO2 equivalent through forest cover by 2030.

India's EV push: FAME II scheme (Rs 10,000 crore subsidies), PLI scheme for advanced chemistry cells (Rs 18,100 crore). India targets 30% EV penetration by 2030. EV sales grew 150% in 2023. Key players: Ola Electric, Tata Motors, Mahindra. National Electric Mobility Mission Plan 2020 set foundational targets.
